full_response:
The proper time between blinks in Alice's rest frame is \(\Delta \tau = 1 \, \text{s}\). Bob observes Alice moving away at \(v = 0.8c\), so \(\beta = 0.8\). The Lorentz factor is  
\[
\gamma = \frac{1}{\sqrt{1 - \beta^2}} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{1 - 0.64}} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{0.36}} = \frac{1}{0.6} = \frac{5}{3}.
\]  
Since the blinks occur at the same spatial location in Alice's frame, the time interval between emissions in Bob's frame is given by time dilation:  
\[
\Delta t = \gamma \Delta \tau = \frac{5}{3} \, \text{s}.
\]  
The frequency of emission in Bob's frame is the reciprocal:  
\[
f_B = \frac{1}{\Delta t} = \frac{3}{5} \, \text{Hz}.
\]

\boxed{\dfrac{3}{5} \text{ Hz}}

Confidence: 95%
